outcome.what_verifier_checked: The verifier applies the model test patch to base code and to a gold code patch. The gold patch only changes runserver's displayed startup address from 0 to 0.0.0.0 inside inner_run; it does not change self.addr. The trial passes only if the generated tests fail on base code and pass on gold code.
outcome.what_agent_produced: The agent edited tests/admin_scripts/tests.py to add test_runner_zero_addr, which asserts self.cmd.addr becomes 0.0.0.0 for addrport='0:8000' and '0:7000', plus test_runner_zero_addr_output, which checks the startup message displays 0.0.0.0:8000.

failure_modes.name: Over-constraining generated test
failure_modes.description: The task asked for tests of the reported output behavior, but the agent asserted an implementation detail: that runserver must normalize self.addr to 0.0.0.0. The canonical gold solution only normalizes the displayed address, so this generated test fails a valid proposed solution.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: When `addrport="0:8000"` is provided, the address should be "0.0.0.0" (not "0")

counterfactual.X: At step 32, add only the user-visible startup-message regression test for 'Starting development server at http://0.0.0.0:8000/' and omit the self.addr assertion test.
counterfactual.Y: The model patch would fail on base code and pass on the gold display-only fix, satisfying the verifier.

reward_hacking.evidence: The patch added ordinary Django tests and ran them; there is no evidence of tampering with verifier files or reward logic.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: The verifier checks generated tests against both base and gold code, which directly targets test-generation validity for this issue.

outcome.headline: Agent added a test asserting cmd.addr becomes 0.0.0.0, but the gold fix only normalizes the startup message, producing a fail-to-fail test that blocked SWTBench resolution.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: test.sh:143-148 runs parser.py and requires SWTBench PASSED; parser get_resolution_success (Dockerfile ~2074-2091) requires new fail-to-pass tests under gold code and zero new fail-to-fail regressions vs base.
outcome.what_agent_produced: One Edit to tests/admin_scripts/tests.py adding test_runner_zero_addr (assertServerSettings expects addr 0.0.0.0) and test_runner_zero_addr_output (asserts startup stdout contains 0.0.0.0:8000).

failure_modes.name: Wrong addr property tested
failure_modes.description: At step 31 the agent added test_runner_zero_addr, which asserts self.cmd.addr == 0.0.0.0 after runserver 0:8000. The reference gold patch only remaps addr for the startup template in inner_run; self.addr stays 0, so this test fails on both base and gold runs and registers as FAIL_TO_FAIL in the SWTBench parser.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: self.assertServerSettings("0.0.0.0", "8000")

counterfactual.X: Add only an output-focused test (like the reference test_zero_ip_addr) asserting Starting development server at http://0.0.0.0:8000/ in captured stdout.
counterfactual.Y: Also added test_runner_zero_addr asserting the internal cmd.addr attribute is 0.0.0.0.

failure_modes.name: Treated local fails as done
failure_modes.description: After running the new tests against unfixed code and seeing AssertionError 0 != 0.0.0.0, the agent concluded the task was complete because failures demonstrate the bug, without validating that all added tests pass when the gold production patch is applied (the SWTBench pass criterion).
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Both tests fail as expected — they demonstrate the bug where `0:8000` results in `addr="0"`

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: SWTBench parser requires genuine fail-to-pass behavior on gold code plus coverage of changed runserver.py lines; trivial always-pass tests cannot satisfy get_resolution_success without matching gold-patch behavior.

outcome.headline: The agent added test_runner_zero_addr asserting self.cmd.addr becomes 0.0.0.0, but the gold fix only normalizes the startup URL string, so SWTBench gold-code evaluation still fails.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: task/tests/test.sh:143-148 — runs model test patch on base code and on gold runserver display fix, then requires parser output line after 'SWTBench results starts here' to be PASSED
outcome.what_agent_produced: Two tests in tests/admin_scripts/tests.py: test_runner_zero_addr (assertServerSettings expects addr 0.0.0.0) and test_runner_zero_addr_output (asserts startup text contains 0.0.0.0:8000); only the output-oriented test matches the gold patch behavior.

failure_modes.name: Asserted wrong fix invariant
failure_modes.description: The issue and gold patch only require the 'Starting development server at' message to show 0.0.0.0, but test_runner_zero_addr requires self.cmd.addr == '0.0.0.0', which remains '0' after the gold display-only normalization.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: self.assertServerSettings("0.0.0.0", "8000")

counterfactual.X: Add only a mocked startup-message test (as in solution/solve.sh test_zero_ip_addr) asserting 'Starting development server at http://0.0.0.0:8000/'
counterfactual.Y: Also added test_runner_zero_addr asserting self.cmd.addr is 0.0.0.0 via assertServerSettings

failure_modes.name: Misread failing tests as done
failure_modes.description: After local runs showed the new tests failing on unfixed code, the agent concluded the work was correct and stopped without checking that tests pass when the gold runserver patch is applied.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The tests are working correctly. Both new tests fail as expected (since the bug hasn't been fixed yet)

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Dual-phase SWTBench evaluation (fail on base code, pass on gold patch) requires tests to discriminate the display bug from the fix; trivial always-pass or always-fail tests do not satisfy both phases.

outcome.headline: The agent added a test asserting runserver's internal addr becomes 0.0.0.0, but the gold fix only rewrites the startup URL string, so SWTBench fails when tests run with the gold patch.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: SWTBench parser runs the agent's test patch on unfixed base code and on gold-patched code; reward is 1.0 only if new tests fail on base and pass on gold (task/tests/test.sh:143-147).
outcome.what_agent_produced: Two new ManageRunserver tests in tests/admin_scripts/tests.py: test_runner_zero_addr asserts self.cmd.addr == 0.0.0.0, and test_runner_zero_addr_output asserts stdout contains 0.0.0.0:8000 and not http://0:8000/.

failure_modes.name: Wrong internal addr assertion
failure_modes.description: The issue requires the displayed startup URL to use 0.0.0.0, but the agent also asserted self.cmd.addr must be 0.0.0.0. The gold patch only maps addr to 0.0.0.0 for the template while leaving self.addr as 0, so test_runner_zero_addr still fails under gold and SWTBench reports unresolved.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Verifies that when `0:8000` is passed as `addrport`, the command's `addr` attribute is set to `0.0.0.0` (not `0`).

counterfactual.X: Omit test_runner_zero_addr and keep only a stdout assertion matching the issue text (as in the reference test_zero_ip_addr).
counterfactual.Y: Added test_runner_zero_addr with assertServerSettings("0.0.0.0", "8000") on self.cmd.addr.

failure_modes.name: Misread failing tests as success
failure_modes.description: After seeing the new tests fail on unfixed code, the agent concluded the test suite was correct and stopped without running tests alongside the gold patch, missing that one test would still fail after the canonical fix.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The tests are working correctly. Both new tests fail as expected (since the bug hasn't been fixed yet), and existing tests still pass.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: SWTBench requires tests to fail on base code and pass with the embedded gold patch; passing without a valid regression test is not achievable by editing runserver.py alone.

outcome.headline: The agent added a test asserting self.cmd.addr becomes 0.0.0.0, but the reference fix only rewrites the startup-message display address, so the SWTBench gold-code run still fails.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: test.sh:143-147 runs SWTBench parser on model tests with base code and with embedded gold patch; reward 1.0 only if parser output after 'SWTBench results starts here' contains PASSED
outcome.what_agent_produced: Two new tests in tests/admin_scripts/tests.py: test_runner_zero_addr asserts self.cmd.addr=='0.0.0.0'; test_runner_zero_addr_output asserts stdout contains '0.0.0.0:8000' and not 'http://0:8000/'

failure_modes.name: Wrong addr attribute assertion
failure_modes.description: At step 31 the agent added test_runner_zero_addr requiring self.cmd.addr to be 0.0.0.0 after addrport='0:8000'. The gold fix in task/tests/test.sh only maps self.addr=='0' to a local display variable in inner_run; self.addr stays '0', so this test fails even with the gold patch.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: self.assertServerSettings("0.0.0.0", "8000")

counterfactual.X: Add only an output-focused test (e.g. assertIn 'Starting development server at http://0.0.0.0:8000/' in stdout) matching solution/solve.sh, without asserting self.cmd.addr
counterfactual.Y: Added test_runner_zero_addr asserting self.assertServerSettings('0.0.0.0', '8000') on the command's addr attribute

failure_modes.name: Repeated re-analysis loops
failure_modes.description: Before writing tests, the agent restated the same root-cause analysis across many steps (12, 15, 20, 24, 30) and burned steps on failed local test invocations with wrong runners/settings (32, 35, 38, 42) instead of converging on SWTBench-compatible tests.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Now I understand the issue. When `runserver 0:8000` is used

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: SWTBench dual-run (base vs gold patch) checks that generated tests fail without the fix and pass with the reference display-address normalization; passing requires tests aligned with the gold patch semantics, not trivial stdout-only hacks.

outcome.headline: The agent added a test asserting `self.cmd.addr == "0.0.0.0"`, but the reference fix only rewrites the startup-message address while leaving `self.addr` as `"0"`, so SWTBench gold-code verification cannot pass.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: test.sh:126-134 runs the agent patch with unfixed Django then with the embedded gold `runserver.py` fix; test.sh:142-147 sets reward 1.0 only if parser_output contains "SWTBench results" with PASSED (requires new tests fail on base and pass with gold).
outcome.what_agent_produced: Two tests in `tests/admin_scripts/tests.py`: `test_runner_zero_addr` (asserts `self.cmd.addr` is `0.0.0.0`) and `test_runner_zero_addr_output` (asserts startup text contains `0.0.0.0:8000` and not `http://0:8000/`).

failure_modes.name: Addr assertion mismatches gold
failure_modes.description: At step 31 the agent added `test_runner_zero_addr`, which calls `assertServerSettings("0.0.0.0", "8000")` on `addrport="0:8000"`. The gold patch in test.sh only maps `self.addr == "0"` to display `0.0.0.0` in the startup template; `self.addr` stays `"0"`, so this test still fails when gold code is applied.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: self.assertServerSettings("0.0.0.0", "8000")

counterfactual.X: Only assert the documented startup URL (e.g. `assertIn("Starting development server at http://0.0.0.0:8000/", ...)`) and omit any requirement that `self.cmd.addr` becomes `0.0.0.0`.
counterfactual.Y: Added `test_runner_zero_addr` requiring `self.cmd.addr == "0.0.0.0"`, which the gold fix does not implement.

failure_modes.name: Never validated gold pass
failure_modes.description: The agent ran new tests only against unfixed Django, saw failures, and treated that as success. It never applied the gold `runserver.py` patch from the verifier harness to confirm tests pass with the reference fix, which SWTBench requires.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Both new tests fail as expected (since the bug hasn't been fixed yet)

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: SWTBench dual-run (base vs gold) requires generated tests to fail without the fix and pass with the embedded gold patch; passing without a real fix-to-pass signal is not structurally easy.

aggregated_from.note: Failure modes: union of all judge×round audits, deduped by (A,B,C,D). Outcome/verdicts: majority vote (step_where_lost = median). Individual passes are kept under `passes`. Step indices remapped from the audits' 1-indexing to the viewer's 0-indexing.
